reasoning: Northern Pacific jet stream asymmetry (westerlies faster than return flow) is a first-principles consequence of Coriolis force acting on Hadley/Ferrel cell circulation on a rotating sphere. Eastbound trans-Pacific flights have been documented as faster since the 1950s. The dome relabels prevailing westerlies as "aetheric currents" but adds no novel predictive content — the asymmetry magnitude and seasonality are fully explained by standard atmospheric dynamics. Data predates registration by decades.
